title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's signal is less about new frontier-model releases and more about the infrastructure around trustworthy deployment: secure evaluation, assurance, fairness measurement, and vulnerability disclosure. The strongest updates come from standards and policy-adjacent organizations, with a smaller number of fresh research papers addressing concrete failure modes such as backdoors and misleading aggregate fairness metrics.

items.title: NIST CAISI signs CRADA with OpenMined for secure AI evaluations
items.why_it_matters: It points to privacy-preserving evaluation infrastructure for testing advanced models without broadly exposing sensitive systems or data.

items.title: Partnership on AI examines whether assurance can make AI systems more trustworthy
items.why_it_matters: Assurance frameworks could shape how organizations evidence safety, governance, and risk controls before deployment.

items.title: Google outlines responsible disclosure of quantum risks to cryptocurrency
items.why_it_matters: It highlights coordinated vulnerability disclosure practices relevant to AI-era security and critical digital infrastructure resilience.

items.title: Study argues aggregate accuracy is inadequate for fairness evaluation in law-enforcement facial recognition
items.why_it_matters: It reinforces that headline accuracy can mask harmful subgroup failures in high-stakes biometric deployments.

items.title: FL-PBM proposes pre-training backdoor mitigation for federated learning
items.why_it_matters: Backdoor defenses matter for distributed training settings where poisoned client data can silently alter model behavior.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
